feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only the provided timing support information is used.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps normal-mode operation.
Apply I2C activity on SCL and SDA at the normal-mode rate.
expected_results: The core operates correctly at 100 Kbps normal-mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only the provided timing support information is used.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps fast-mode operation.
Apply I2C activity on SCL and SDA at the fast-mode rate.
expected_results: The core operates correctly at 400 Kbps fast-mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the core exposes a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_constraints: Compliance is asserted from the interface definition provided in the specification.
test_steps: Connect the core to a WISHBONE Classic environment using the listed WISHBONE signals.
Perform a standard WISHBONE access sequence.
expected_results: The interface operates as a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
The listed WISHBONE signals are used for bus access and interrupt signaling.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that each WISHBONE access takes two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Access timing is evaluated using the WISHBONE clock and bus handshake signals.
test_steps: Initiate a WISHBONE access with wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i asserted.
Measure the number of wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
expected_results: wb_ack_o is asserted after two clock cycles.
Each access completes in two clock cycles.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset behavior when ARST_LVL is configured active high.
feature_list.test_constraints: The reset polarity configuration is limited to the value described in the requirement.
test_steps: Assert arst_i high and observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: The core recognizes arst_i high as the active reset level.
Asynchronous reset is applied with active-high polarity.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset behavior when ARST_LVL is configured active low.
feature_list.test_constraints: The reset polarity configuration is limited to the value described in the requirement.
test_steps: Assert arst_i low and observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: The core recognizes arst_i low as the active reset level.
Asynchronous reset is applied with active-low polarity.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the core exposes SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only the pad signal presence and direction information is used.
test_steps: Check the external connection list for SCL pad signals.
Observe the listed SCL pad input, output, and output-enable interfaces.
expected_results: scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe are present.
The SCL external bus connection supports input, output, and output-enable signals.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the core exposes SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only the pad signal presence and direction information is used.
test_steps: Check the external connection list for SDA pad signals.
Observe the listed SDA pad input, output, and output-enable interfaces.
expected_results: sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe are present.
The SDA external bus connection supports input, output, and output-enable signals.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
